findings.title: Duplicate source-to-text mapping on lines 131 and 174
findings.description: The pattern `.map(s => \`[${s.source}] ${s.summary}\`).join('\n')` appears identically at line 131 (in merge()) and line 174 (in generateSummary()). The merge method builds combinedText, then generateSummary rebuilds the exact same string from the same sources. This means generateSummary could accept the already-built combinedText string instead of rebuilding it.

findings.title: Unreachable catch guard for empty-sources error
findings.description: The empty-sources guard (`throw new Error('At least one context source is required')`) is on line 121, which is BEFORE the try block starts on line 124. Therefore this error can never be caught by the catch block. The string comparison on line 152 is dead code -- it will never match.

findings.title: extractEntities is a single-line passthrough method
findings.description: The private method `extractEntities` is a trivial single-line delegation to `this.entityExtractor.extract(text)`. Unlike `classifyActivity` and `generateSummary` which contain LLM call construction logic, this method adds no transformation, no error handling, and no additional parameters. It could be inlined at the call site for slightly less indirection.

positive_observations: Clean separation between pure Functional Core (deduplicateStrings, mergeKeywords, computeImportance, selectTopSources) and Imperative Shell (ContextMerger class), following the project's architecture conventions.
All functions are short and focused: the longest (merge) is ~38 lines, well under the 50-line threshold. Cyclomatic complexity is low (1-4) across all functions.
Named constants (DEFAULT_MAX_SOURCES, DEFAULT_IMPORTANCE, VALID_ACTIVITY_TYPES) used consistently instead of magic values.
Guard clauses and early returns are used effectively throughout (empty array checks, type validation fallback).
Good use of Promise.all for concurrent LLM calls, avoiding unnecessary sequential await chains.
Test files are well-organized with focused describe blocks and appropriate test double rationale comments for the LLM boundary.
